title: Wet-intermediate gated modeling
summary: Published pit-stop modeling work notes that dry tyre strategy is predictable from degradation and track position, while wet/intermediate stops are more reactive and noisy.
rationale: Published pit-stop modeling work notes that dry tyre strategy is predictable from degradation and track position, while wet/intermediate stops are more reactive and noisy. The dataset includes Compound, so one global model may blur regimes.
implementation_hint: Train either separate dry and wet/intermediate models or add a regime gate feature with interactions: Is_Dry, Is_WetLike, Compound_Ordinal within regime, wet-specific tyre-life bins, and separate hazard priors. Blend predictions by regime rather than forcing one shared hazard curve.
expected_effect: May reduce false positives/negatives for INTERMEDIATE/WET rows and improve ranking within rare but strategically different regimes.
risk: Wet/intermediate sample size may be small; separate models can overfit unless heavily regularized or blended back toward the global model.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
